---
title: "Weizheng Bai（白炜政）"
subtitle: "PhD Student in Civil Engineering | Underground Hydrogen Storage"
description: "Weizheng Bai（白炜政） researches underground hydrogen storage, salt cavern geomechanics, hydrogen seepage, leakage safety, and intelligent energy storage."
keywords: [Weizheng Bai, 白炜政, underground hydrogen storage, salt cavern, geomechanics, hydrogen seepage, energy storage]
image: images/bio-photo.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  image-width: 13em
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: mailbox
      text: baiweizheng22@mails.ucas.ac.cn
      href: mailto:baiweizheng22@mails.ucas.ac.cn
    - icon: book
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=vqyiqNgAAAAJ&hl=zh-CN&oi=ao
    - icon: fingerprint
      text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0009-0000-6673-3838
    - icon: sina-weibo
      text: 微博
      href: https://www.weibo.com/u/7817044578
    - icon: heart
      text: 小红书
      href: https://www.xiaohongshu.com/user/profile/5e5fb0a70000000001007a88?xsec_token=AB3GLf7TV9coqTZ78EIzCEQmd9ubKKUJRaP2d9uN2umB8%3D&xsec_source=pc_search
    - icon: file-earmark-person
      text: CV
      href: https://lesliewhitebai.github.io/Weizheng-Bai/CV-Weizheng%20Bai.pdf
---

I am a researcher in civil and geotechnical engineering at the Ineris - Institut national de l'environnement industriel et des risques. My work focuses on the safe, large-scale use of underground geological formations for hydrogen and other forms of energy storage. I combine multiphysics simulation, geomechanics, site evaluation, and risk assessment to study how salt caverns can support renewable energy systems.

## Research Focus

::: {.research-grid}
::: {.research-card}
### Underground hydrogen storage

Storage capacity, operating performance, and engineering feasibility of salt caverns and other geological structures.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Hydrogen leakage & safety

Hydrogen transport through surrounding rock, well integrity, containment performance, and leakage risk assessment.
:::

::: {.research-card}
### Intelligent energy storage

Artificial intelligence and data-driven methods for site screening, suitability evaluation, and operational decision-making.
:::
:::

## Selected Publications

1. **Bai, W.**, Shi, X., Yang, C., et al. (2024). Assessment of the potential of salt mines for renewable energy peaking in China. *Energy*, 300, 131577. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.energy.2024.131577)
2. **Bai, W.**, Shi, X., Zhu, S., et al. (2025). Opportunities and challenges of large-scale salt cavern hydrogen storage in China coupled with renewable energy sources. *Clean Energy*, 9(1), 179-196. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1093/ce/zkae095)
3. **Bai, W.**, Shi, X., Zhu, S., Wei, X., & Huang, Y. (2025). Leakage risk assessment system for salt cavern hydrogen storage. *Deep Underground Science and Engineering*. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1002/dug2.70011)
4. Zhu, S., Shi, X., Yang, C., **Bai, W.**, et al. (2024). Site selection evaluation for salt cavern hydrogen storage in China. *Renewable Energy*, 224, 120143. [DOI](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.renene.2024.120143)

[View all publications](publications.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}

## Research Experience

**National Key R&D Program of China: Applicability and Key Safety Technologies of Geological Hydrogen Storage Facilities**<br>
*December 2024 - November 2027*

Developing evaluation indices for geological sealing and stability, integrating geological structure, rock properties, chamber geometry, stress fields, formation fluids, and environmental constraints to support favorable-block selection.

**Research and Development of Key Technologies for Underground Hydrogen Storage**<br>
*January 2023 - December 2024*

Studied hydrogen transport in surrounding rock, established a geological suitability evaluation system, and screened feasible geological sites for underground hydrogen storage in China.

## Education

| Period | Degree and institution |
|---|---|
| 2026 - Present | PhD student in Structures and Materials, Ineris - Institut national de l'environnement industriel et des risques，France |
| 2022 - 2025 | MSc in Civil and Hydraulic Engineering, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, GPA 3.69/4.0 |
| 2022 - 2023 | Non-degree coursework in Civil and Hydraulic Engineering, Wuhan University |
| 2017 - 2021 | BEng in Civil Engineering, Shandong University of Science and Technology |

## Skills and Recognition

**Methods and software:** COMSOL Multiphysics, FLAC3D, MATLAB, Origin, Microsoft Office<br>
**Languages:** Chinese (native), English (IELTS 6.5)

- China National Scholarship, 2024 (top 1%)
- Outstanding Graduate, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, 2025
- Outstanding Student, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences, 2024
- First-class Award Scholarship, Graduate School of UCAS, 2022-2024

## Academic Service

Reviewer for journals including *Tunnelling and Underground Space Technology*, *Energy*, *Fuel*, *Energy Strategy Reviews*, *Journal of Rock Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering*, and *Journal of Energy Storage*.
